issues.title: Flutter TextFields on Chrome and Edge (but not Windows or Firefox) receive multiple onChange events from Japanese input
issues.body:
When programatically shifting focus between TextFields as part of an TextField's onChange event triggered by Japanese IME input, the change event seems to be incorrectly received by multiple TextFields. The behaviour is not consistent between Windows and Firefox on one side, and Chrome and Edge on the other.  The behaviour can also be avoided by forcing a delay in the input handling code, indicating a possible race.

## Steps to Reproduce
A simplified application has two TextFields, "left" and "right". Each has an onChanged handler. Left's onChange handler prints the received text to the console, and if the input text was "t" or "れ", gives focus to right.  Right's onChange handler prints the received text to the console.

DartPad: https://dartpad.dev/?id=484f57a4221afe3ffced3614a23f1316

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
1 - I clear both fields and paste "t" into the left field. Focus shifts to the right field, the left field shows "t":
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48515264/175526037-887c0069-6f1e-4d18-8f8f-cbf25e063fe6.png)
Console shows:
`leftField onChange called with t`

2 - I clear both fields and type "t" into the left field. Focus shifts to the right field, the left field shows "t":
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48515264/175525999-f8d7de67-2ffd-42a4-bfdb-7af08049a59b.png)
Console shows:
`leftField onChange called with t`

3 - I clear both fields and paste "れ" into the left field. Focus shifts to the right field, the left field shows "れ":
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48515264/175525963-1f909acb-7cc1-4cc6-8161-2cf0bb720cd7.png)
Console shows:
`leftField onChange called with れ`

4 - I clear both fields and write "れ" into the left field using my IME. Focus shifts to the right field, the left field shows "れ":
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48515264/175525671-e1a4be8d-ccd8-4cc1-8929-cdaba91590e3.png)
Console shows:
`leftField onChange called with r`
`leftField onChange called with れ`

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
On Windows, I see the desired behaviour (as shown in the screenshots above). 

On DartPad in Firefox, I see the expected behaviour, but during test 4 I receive an additional line on the console:
`leftField onChange called with r`
`leftField onChange called with れ`
`Error: Unexpected null value.Error: Error: Unexpected null value.`

I am unsure where this message is coming from, as no traceback is provided and I cannot attach a debugger to DartPad.

On Chrome, I see the expected behaviour for tests 1, 2, and 3. For test 4, I end up in this state:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48515264/175526682-41ed3122-d3f8-462f-a8fd-4a3caca5fff8.png)
There is an additional line in the console:
`leftField onChange called with r`
`leftField onChange called with れ`
`rightField onChange called with れ`

The right field should be blank, and should not contain 'れ' - the user entered no text there. rightField's onChange event should not have been called.

On Edge, I see the expected behaviour for tests 1, 2, and 3. For test 4, I end up in this state:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48515264/175527146-b7a165e0-d9cf-43a1-8b2c-209d246b8be1.png)
There is an additional line in the console:
`leftField onChange called with ｒ`
`leftField onChange called with れ`
`rightField onChange called with れ`

As with the Chrome test, the right field should be blank, and should not contain 'れ'. rightField's onChange event should not have been called.

## Commentary
It seems that the original event is somehow persisting and that after the right hand TextField receives focus, it also receives that same event. Unfortunately, I have been unable to make much sense of the stack here to understand how TextFields receive their input (and from where), but with the program showing the correct behaviour for roman characters and pasted Japanese input, it would seem that the issue is something to do with the synthesised nature of IME input.

There is also potentially a race condition involved here (which might also explain the Firefox situation - it may be attempting to do the same thing as Chrome / Edge, but with part of the event having been cleaned up to null by the time it attempts to re-transmit it.) If a conditional breakpoint is inserted into editable_text.dart's _formatAndSetValue (set to only take effect when the input is 'れ' to avoid it triggering on the first 'r'), the pause and unpause caused by the debugger removes the behaviour:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48515264/175542597-d473564b-8680-4989-b154-0c39bc1c78ad.mp4

Thanks for reading.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(
      title: 'Test Focus',
      home: MyHomePage(title: 'Test Focus'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  final String title;
  const MyHomePage({
    Key? key,
    required this.title,
  }) : super(key: key);
  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    FocusNode focusReceiver = FocusNode();

    final leftField = TextField(
        maxLength: 3,
        onChanged: (text) {
          print('leftField onChange called with $text');
          if (text == 't' || text == 'れ') {
            focusReceiver.requestFocus();
          }
        });

    final rightField = TextField(
        focusNode: focusReceiver,
        maxLength: 3,
        onChanged: (text) {
          print('rightField onChange called with $text');
        });

    return Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(title: Text(widget.title)),
        body: Row(children: [
          Expanded(child: leftField),
          const VerticalDivider(),
          Expanded(child: rightField)
        ]));
  }
}
```
</details>

comments:
Hi @curiousjp, thanks for filing the issue.
I'm quite confused about the issue description. I was executing your example code on Windows with Chrome and Firefox, and below is a demo:

| Chrome | Firefox|
| --------------- | --------------- |
<video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/104349824/175484792-aee0e6ea-a509-4aff-a4f9-b7e8ede62b65.mp4"/> | <video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/104349824/175484820-38244ad5-4ef4-4259-b83e-1e4e9fef0778.mp4"/>

> When entering "れ" via the IME (i.e. typing 'r' 'e') in the top left TextField, its onChange handler should be invoked twice with the arguments 'r' and then 'れ'. Each invocation should be logged to the console. As part of the second invocation, focus should shift to the next TextField in the column.

On Firefox, it had an error:
>ｒ
れ
Error: Unexpected null value.Error: Error: Unexpected null value.

So please update the description and point out the bug that belongs to Flutter, and it should be as minimal as possible (removing extraneous stuff)

For example, there are some questions you can refer to:
- Does the issue occur with Hiragana or Roman? (If it's both, update the comparison. If it's only one of them, leave the other out in the description.)
- Do you think the problem is with Flutter's TextField or FocusNode? Or is it just a logic issue?

Thank you!

Hi @huycozy  - thank you for taking the time to look at the report and the example code. Following your advice, I have rewritten the original issue with a narrower example program and some additional information I have been able to put together. I hope this is easier to understand, and that the change in behaviour when inserting a delay gives someone a hint as to what might be going on.

@curiousjp 
Thank you for the detailed issue description (it's so useful).
I can reproduce this issue on the latest `stable` and `master` channels.

The issue:  TextField's onChange event (triggered by Japanese IME input) was incorrectly received with `FocusNode`

- Chrome: ❌
- Windows: ✅
- Firefox: ✅ (but has an error in console)

✅: No Issue ❌: Issue reproduced

<details>
<summary>Demo</summary>

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/104349824/175886569-0cf8e733-2f2b-437f-829d-6e5d601d71e8.mp4

</details>

<details>
<summary>Sample code</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(
      title: 'Test Focus',
      home: MyHomePage(title: 'Test Focus'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  final String title;
  const MyHomePage({
    Key? key,
    required this.title,
  }) : super(key: key);
  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    FocusNode focusReceiver = FocusNode();

    final leftField = TextField(
        maxLength: 3,
        onChanged: (text) {
          print('leftField onChange called with $text');
          if (text == 't' || text == 'れ') {
            focusReceiver.requestFocus();
          }
        });

    final rightField = TextField(
        focusNode: focusReceiver,
        maxLength: 3,
        onChanged: (text) {
          print('rightField onChange called with $text');
        });

    return Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(title: Text(widget.title)),
        body: Row(children: [
          Expanded(child: leftField),
          const VerticalDivider(),
          Expanded(child: rightField)
        ]));
  }
}

```
</details>

issues.title: TextField autoFocus not working in Android Profile or Release builds
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce 1. Run `flutter create bug`. 2. Update the files as follows: Insert an autoFocus TextField widget into the existing column, as in: ```dart mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, children: [ TextField( autofocus: true, ), Text( 'You have pushed the button this many times:', ), ``` 3. Run the app on Android in all configurations: debug, profiling, and release. **Expected results:** The keyboard should automatically appear when the app launches. This is currently working for the debug build: **Actual results:** On Profile and Release builds, the input cursor appears in the TextField, but the keboard does not automatically open: The user must manually tap inside the field in order to open the keyboard.

Have seen similar issues in web, the behavior is just in release mode AFAIK (not tested profile). Works most of the times in Chrome, but more significant in Edge. We are at the moment in stable 3.24.1 and beta 3.25.0-0.1.pre.
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.
Found that if you hit F5 ("soft-reload") this issue may disappear in MS Edge. Can provoke it when running `flutter run --release` targeting MSEdge, not Chrome.
Note, the only remedy to gain focus is using the `Tab` key on the keyboard. Mouse click won't work either, even if the caret is blinking.

Recently tested in 
Flutter beta v3.26.0-0.1.pre with `flutter run --release` targeting MS Edge: Issue exists.
Flutter master v3.26-1.0.pre.119 (revision 2812d4685c 2024-09-13 13:53:05 +0200): Isse DON'T exists.

Not sure why this should work in master, but not beta, I'll see if I am able to publish a test on *.codemagic.app where I have seen similar issues before.
Published my app on *.codemagic.app using master channel, still an issue both on Chrome and MS Edge !!

Falling back to Flutter Beta, when I couldn't see any apparent difference between channels used.

Observations using `flutter run --release` targeting MS Edge (where it mostly happens, it do happen in Chrome browser also, but not so frequent):

1. The appearance of the bug is random
2. It may or may not appear of you hit F5 (soft reload)
3. It is more likely to happen when you have published your app (i.e. on *.codemagic.app)
4. It is less (or not at all) likely to happen of you run your app in developer mode.

If the platform thread is merged with the UI thread, this likely won't be a problem anymore. I'm basing this on the [comment above](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/59439#issuecomment-645061575) that says this is likely a race condition.

issues.title: [chromeOS] TextField backspace doesn't work properly and text select cannot be selected with Japaense input
issues.body:
My application user report this problem and I don't have ChomeOs device.
So, Information may be lacking, but please forgive me.

This is the minimum program
```
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class SamplePage extends StatefulWidget {

  SamplePage(): super();

  @override
  SamplePageState createState() => SamplePageState();
}

class SamplePageState extends State<SamplePage> with WidgetsBindingObserver {

  Widget build(BuildContext context){
    // GoogleFonts.kosugi().fontFamily
    Widget bodyScaffold = Scaffold(
      body: Container(
        padding: EdgeInsets.only(top:50),
        child: TextField(),
      ),
    );

    return bodyScaffold;
  }
}
```

This problem occur at Chromebook(Lenovo IdeaPad Duet)

# Problem 1
Backspace key does not work depending on some situation.
- BackSpace key works when Inputing japanese.
- BackSpace key doesn't work when characters after confirmation

This is the video reported from user
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7422251/111177488-9c3e3d00-85ed-11eb-9126-db06731c520c.mp4

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Input japanese
2. confim text
3. press backspace

**Expected results:**
backspace works and character will be delete

**Actual results:** 
backspace doesn't works and character doesn't delete

# Problem 2 
Fail to select character with shift button in textfield doesn't work

This is the video reported from user
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7422251/111177938-09ea6900-85ee-11eb-9f26-414c3c9e30f7.mp4

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Input japanese
2. confim text
3. select text using shift button( shift + arrowKey )

**Expected results:**
Can select Character

**Actual results:** 
Cannot select Character and cursor only move

issues.title: [macOS][Desktop]: Entering a compound character crashes Mac app
issues.body:
I'm working on IME support for Super Editor. At the moment, if I try to enter a `ü` in an empty paragraph, the entire Mac app crashes.

It's likely that this problem is the result of something that we're doing in Super Editor, however, I'm thinking that shouldn't be possible to completely crash the app. So something should probably be updated on the Flutter side, too.

The key presses to generate a `ü` are:
* opt+u
* u

Here's a snippet from the Mac crash report that results from this crash:

comments: CC @justinmc

Thanks for the report @matthew-carroll.
I tried to replicate this behavior by cloning `super_editor` and ran `example` on macOS. Used German locale and tried entering compound char (using option + u) but it doesn't seem to render that char. Although it renders Japanese and other locales.

I selected `Pieces -> Paragraphs` from menu bar, cleared the existing text and then tried above. Maybe I properly didn't replicate it or missed some steps to exactly replicate it.

Can you please provide / confirm steps to replicate this crash ?

Here's a video that demonstrates the crash:


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7259036/170211751-3e9d7e51-b4cf-4114-b985-1058f28906a7.mov

I don't think you need the German keyboard. Even on the American keyboard, I can type "opt+u" and then "u" and cause the crash.

Thanks for the video. It helped to replicate the crash.

Probably related bug: entering a compound at the beginning of a non-empty paragraph produces weird result.

1. Move the cursor to the end of a non-empty paragraph (such as `Foo`)
2. Press Option-U, then u

Expected result: `üFoo`

Actual result: `¨Füo`
> jumpto butten

The bug is caused by super_editor's internal call `_DocumentImeInteractorState.currentTextEditingValue` setting an invalid `TextEditingValue` whose composing range is out of text. This error is not caught during construction, because `TextEditingValue` is a const class and can't verify `TextRange`'s fields. 

To catch this on the framework side, I tried to add verification to `toJSON` and `fromJSON`. However, this is not sufficient. The error occurred during a method call handler, where errors are swallowed and converted to an error reply to the method call. Unsurprisingly, the engine side doesn't handle errors. Even if it does, the error would be very hard to track due to lack of stacktrace.

Therefore I created https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/104711 that wraps method call handlers with a `try` block and reports errors to `FlutterErrors.report`. Now, when you're pressing `Option-U` at the beginning of a paragraph, the CLI prints:
```
══╡ EXCEPTION CAUGHT BY SERVICES LIBRARY ╞══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
The following assertion was thrown during method call TextInputClient.updateEditingStateWithDeltas:
Range start 2 is out of text of length 1
'package:flutter/src/services/text_input.dart':
Failed assertion: line 952 pos 12: 'range.start >= 0 && range.start <= text.length'

Either the assertion indicates an error in the framework itself, or we should provide substantially
more information in this error message to help you determine and fix the underlying cause.
In either case, please report this assertion by filing a bug on GitHub:
  https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/new?template=2_bug.md

When the exception was thrown, this was the stack:
```

We shoudl fix the macOS embedding side of this to do proper bounds checking so that it doesn't crash on malformed values...
Guarding it with asserts in the framework is a good idea, but that won't help in profile/release mode, and we shouldn't have the application crash in this way.

The framework should no longer let this happen to the embedding - talked to @dkwingsmt and we're going to leave this open to track fixing the embedding so it won't crash, although that can be done at a more leisurely pace since the framework won't send invalid data now.

At any rate, it should be an easy fix in the embedding to just add some bounds checking.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.
